The talk examines how age and sex differences in behavior relate to human life-history evolution.

Address: 99 Gansevoort St, New York, NY 10014, USA

Name: Museum of Chinese in America
Event: MOCA x NYAFF Film Club: American Girl
Description: Screening of the Taiwanese film American Girl (2021) followed by a moderated conversation and a casual reception. Part of the MOCA x NYAFF Film Club series highlighting Asian and Asian American cinema under the theme “Margin.”
Cost: General Admission: $10 | MOCA Member Admission: Free
Address: 215 Centre St, New York, NY 10013, USA

Name: Cooper Hewitt, Smithsonian Design Museum
Description: A sensory-friendly evening of structured activities exploring how music and design intersect, including a 30-minute guided tour, hands-on activities, refreshments, and a quiet break room. Registration is required and the program is free for visitors with disabilities and their companions.
Address: 2 E 91st St, New York, NY 10128, USA

Name: Cooper Hewitt, Smithsonian Design Museum
Description: Explore the Art of Noise exhibition at your own pace with opportunities to discover, connect, and engage with gallery content; trained staff will be available for visual description, close looking, and tactile exploration. Registration is not required but is strongly encouraged; visitors with disabilities and their companions receive free admission with prior registration.
Address: 2 E 91st St, New York, NY 10128, USA

Name: Neue Galerie New York
Event: Sanatorium Under the Sign of the Hourglass (Rescheduled)
Description: A Filmbar screening of The Quay Brothers’ film about Josef’s journey to a labyrinthine sanatorium, presented as part of the Winter/Spring 2026 Filmbar Series “Clinical Studies.” This is a rescheduled screening and advance registration is required.
Cost: $28 - General; $18 - Seniors/Students; Complimentary for Neue Galerie members
Address: 1048 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: Neue Galerie New York
Description: Perlman Music Program recital featuring gifted young musicians performing in Café Sabarsky; the recital provides a welcoming space for artists to explore recital and chamber repertoire. Space is limited and advance registration is required.
Cost: $45.00 (General); Free for members (member ticket option available)
Address: 1048 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: Neue Galerie New York
Event: Free Access to “Egon Schiele: Portrait of Dr. Erwin von Graff” (First Friday)
Description: First Fridays: the museum is open late with free access to the galleries, including the special exhibition Egon Schiele: Portrait of Dr. Erwin von Graff; admission is first-come, first-served. Bookstore, Design Shop, and Café Sabarsky remain open late.
Address: 1048 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: Neue Galerie New York
Event: Private view of “Egon Schiele: Portrait of Dr. Erwin von Graff” (Members’ Thursday)
Description: An exclusive Members’ Thursday evening when the museum is open late for members and their guests with a private viewing of the Egon Schiele special exhibition; a gallery tour is offered at 6:30 p.m. Members receive advance notification and RSVP instructions.
Address: 1048 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: Neue Galerie New York
Description: Lecture presented by Michelle Young about Rose Valland, the curator who secretly documented Nazi art thefts and helped recover stolen artworks after World War II. The talk is part of the Winter/Spring 2026 Lecture Series.
Cost: $15 - General; $10 - Seniors/Students; Complimentary for Neue Galerie members
Address: 1048 5th Ave, New York, NY 10028, USA

Name: Museum of Arts and Design
Event: AIGA NY: Parallels with Tito Deler
Description: A talk presented by AIGA NY exploring creativity across multiple platforms. Tito Deler will share his creative story spanning graphic design, fashion, and music.
Address: 2 Columbus Cir, New York, NY 10019, USA

Name: Museum of Arts and Design
Event: Haas Brothers: Uncanny Valley Opening Preview
Description: A members-only opening preview for the Haas Brothers midcareer survey, featuring welcoming remarks in The Theater and an exhibition preview with reception. Doors open early for seating; exhibition previews and reception follow.
Address: 2 Columbus Cir, New York, NY 10019, USA

Event: The Metropolitan Opera: Kaija Saariaho’s Innocence
Description: A preview performance and moderated discussion of Kaija Saariaho’s opera Innocence presented by Works & Process and The Metropolitan Opera, with cast members performing highlights from the score.
